ocr_mistral.text:
Surgeon General's Office,
Record and Pension Bureau,
Washington, D. C., Oct. 10th, 1867.

Sir:

I have the honor to return herewith application for Pension, No. 152,744 with such information as is furnished by the records of this Office, Private
Haren Chanders, Co. "F", 118th
Regiment U.S.C.T., is reported to this Office by upon records of Surgeon: Hospital in the Office as having died November 6th, 1867, at 18th
Farey Corps Hosp'l.
of "Sickness"
"Overdraft Chapin Farm, Va"

Very respectfully,
Your obedient servant,

BY ORDER OF THE SURGEON GENERAL:

J. J. Woodward
Brevet Major and Assistant Surgeon, U. S. A.

The Commissioner of Pensions,
Washington, D. C.

transcribe_handwriting.text:
Surgeon General's Office,
Record and Pension Bureau,
Washington, D. C. Oct 10th 1867.

Sir:

I have the honor to return herewith application for Pension, No. 156.414,
with such information as is furnished by the records of this Office.

Aaron Saunders, Co. B, 118th
Regiment U.S. Col. is reported to this Office by report of
Surg[r]on? Field, A.A. Genl Officer, as having died December 6, 1864 at
"II Army Corps Hosp. 7 Divis."
of "Chapin" form "V[?]"

Very respectfully,
Your obedient servant,

BY ORDER OF THE SURGEON GENERAL:

J. J. Woodward
Brevet Major and Assistant Surgeon, U.S.A.

The Commissioner of Pensions,
Washington, D. C.
